mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns exceed 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 24-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with greater than 60 percent accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side growth that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDP growth signals expanding corporate revenue bases, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with greater than 52 percent accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent month-over-month are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 58 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day forward returns or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside rising interest rates signal a credit-constrained housing sector that drags on broader economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The HOUST-FEDFUNDS divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 90 percent confidence level in walk-forward tests.
